species.notes: Found on nearly all the islands and island groups of the Philippines, although extirpated in many lowland areas. Mostly in primary forests (including mixed dipterocarp forest, and swamp forest) but also found in secondary forest (e.g. emerging from logged-over forest), quite often found near rivers; coral limestone, clay, and ultramafic rocks. Fl. Feb-May; fr. Mar-Aug; Oct-Dec. Last collected in 1996 (Sohmer & Davis 2007). 50-1500m.

Ind. 2 (1857) 181, nom. illeg., non Walp. ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 500; Backer & Bakh.f., Fl. Java 2 (1963) 287; ≡ Metabolus rigidus Blume , Bijdr. fl. Ned. Ind. (1826-1827) 992; Type: from Java. = Hedyotis gitingensis Elmer , LPB 3 (1911) 979 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 497; Type: Elmer 12434. = Hedyotis pulgarensis Elmer , LPB 4 (1912) 1333 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 499; Type: Elmer 13212 (L, MO, NY, US, iso), Palawan: Puerto Princesa, Mt Pulgar (Thumb Peak), May-1911. ≠ Hedyotis rigida auct. non (Benth.) Walp.
species.distribution: Australia, Bangladesh, Borneo, Himalayas, India, Java, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, Myanmar, New Guinea, Philippines, Sumatra, Thailand. BASILAN, BUSUANGA, CAMIGUIN, CULION, LEYTE (photos), LUZON: Bataan, Benguet, Bulacan, Camarines, Isabela, Laguna, Nueva Vizcaya, Pampanga, Pangasinan, Rizal, Sorsogon, Zambales, MINDANAO: Bukidnon, Lanao, Surigao, MINDORO, NEGROS, PALAWAN, PANAY, POLILLO, SIBUYAN.
species.notes: Throughout the Philippines in most islands and provinces. Chiefly in low and medium elevation forests, thickets and grasslands, to 1000m, often common. Joaquin Almoro (16-Dec-24): the name Hedyotis rigida (Blume) Walp. has been used for this species, but Walpers cited not Metabolus rigidus Blume, but instead Kohautia rigida Benth. as its basionym. The correct name for this African species is therefore Hedyotis rigida (Benth.) Walp. Because this name antedates Hedyotis rigida (Blume) Miq., the latter is illegitimate. The next available name for this Asian/Australian taxon appears to be Hedyotis leucocarpa Elmer. 1200m.
